steps.title: Identify what dipeptides do in soy sauce
steps.content: Text 2 reveals a crucial finding: dipeptides (proteins in soy sauce) enhance umami flavor and also contribute to saltiness. This dual effect is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Apply this discovery to the 2007 experiment
steps.content:
Ishii and O'Mahony used mushroom and seaweed broths specifically to define “pure umami flavor”—isolating umami without interference from other tastes.

If these broths had contained substantial dipeptides, those compounds would have added saltiness alongside the umami, contaminating the pure signal they were seeking.

Therefore, the broths would most likely have lacked substantial dipeptides.

The other choices either make unsupported claims about national diets, propose unclear molecular weight comparisons, or mischaracterize how proteins entered soy sauce.

That is the correct answer.
